name: Barbequed Swordfish with Black Olive-Cucumber Salad
recipeIngredient: 4 swordfish fillets (steaks), 6 ounces each, about 1-inch thick
1 cup black olives such as Gaeta
1 pound English cucumbers (1 long one), sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the barbecue with a good hot fire.
2. Rinse and pat dry the swordfish steaks.
3. In a small mixing bowl, mix tomato sauce, sugar, balsamic vinegar, chili flakes, and let stand.
4. Into another mixing bowl, add Gaeta olives and cucumber. Cube plum tomatoes into 1/8-inch cubes and add to the cucumber mixture. Add 2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, 1 tablespoon red wine vinegar, and 2 tablespoons oregano leaves. Mix well. Do not season until ready to serve or vegetables will wilt.
5. In a blender, mix Dijon mustard, 1/2 cup extra virgin olive oil, and 6 tablespoons red wine vinegar until smooth. Remove the mixture and stir in chopped chives. Set aside.
6. Brush swordfish steaks with barbecue sauce and season with salt and pepper.
7. Cook on the first side for 3 minutes, then flip over. Spoon 1 ounce of barbecue sauce over each steak and finish cooking without turning about 4 more minutes.
8. Meanwhile, divide cucumber salad among 4 plates. When the swordfish is finished, lean one steak on each plate over the salad. Drizzle with chive sauce and serve.
Yield: 4 servings
